methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 12 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $7.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Cerebras
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Pre-IPO), post-money $23B

data.narrative:
Rebellions is a pre-IPO chip design company with $625M+ raised and a stated valuation of $2.34B. The comparable universe spans 12 same-sector peers across Chips & Semiconductors, blending 8 private companies (including PsiQuantum at $7.0B, Etched at $5.0B, and Tenstorrent at $2.7B) and 3 public semiconductors benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Peer-median valuation anchoring is the applicable triangulation method, given Rebellions' private status. The 12-company peer set yields a median valuation of $1.8B, with a range spanning $500M to $7.0B. This method directly addresses Rebellions' position within the frontier-chip competitive landscape. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied: while public semiconductors trade at a median 22.3x P/S, Rebellions' revenue is not disclosed, precluding revenue-based multiples.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.5B–$2.0B (low to high), centered on the peer median of $1.8B. Rebellions' stated $2.34B valuation sits above this range, suggesting either undisclosed revenue inflection, proprietary IP premium relative to peers, or valuation inflation pending market correction. Key uncertainties include unreported financials, technical differentiation versus the peer set, and customer traction—none of which are quantified in available data. The single-method foundation limits conviction; revenue or margin disclosure would enable public-comp triangulation and narrow the range materially.
